# synix

> A build system for agent memory — declarative pipelines define how raw conversations become searchable, hierarchical memory with full provenance tracking.

## What it does

Synix processes conversation exports through configurable DAGs of LLM transforms to create hierarchical memory artifacts (episodes, summaries, rollups, core memory) with full lineage tracking. Change a config, only affected layers rebuild. Think `make` or `dbt`, but for AI agent memory producing system prompt + RAG outputs.

## Key concepts

- **Artifact** — immutable, versioned build output (transcript, episode, rollup, core memory). Content-addressed via SHA256.
- **Layer** — typed Python object in build DAG. `Source` for inputs, `Transform` subclasses for LLM steps, `SearchSurface`/`SynixSearch`/`FlatFile` for projections.
- **Pipeline** — declared in Python. `Pipeline.add(*layers)` routes transforms to layers, projections to manifest.
- **Projection** — structured declaration materialized at release time by adapters (`synix_search`, `flat_file`).
- **Release** — `synix release HEAD --to <name>` materializes projections from snapshot into `.synix/releases/<name>/`.
- **Provenance** — every artifact traces back via `parent_labels`. Always included in search results.
- **Cache/Rebuild** — hash comparison: if inputs or prompt changed, rebuild. Otherwise skip.

## Installation and quick start

```bash
# Install and initialize project
uvx synix init my-project
cd my-project

# Build immutable snapshot
uvx synix build

# Materialize projections (search.db, context.md) 
uvx synix release HEAD --to local

# Search and inspect
uvx synix search "query" --release local
uvx synix list
uvx synix show artifact-label
```

## CLI commands

- `synix build pipeline.py` — produce immutable snapshot in `.synix/`
- `synix plan pipeline.py` — dry-run showing rebuild/cached decisions
- `synix release HEAD --to local` — materialize projections to named release
- `synix search "query" --release local` — search release target
- `synix list [--ref HEAD]` — artifacts in snapshot via SnapshotView
- `synix show <label> [--ref HEAD]` — render artifact content
- `synix lineage <label>` — provenance tree from `.synix/objects/`
- `synix releases list/show <name>` — release history and receipts
- `synix refs list/show <ref>` — all refs with snapshot details
- `synix clean` — remove release targets and work state
- `synix verify` — (experimental) run validators against artifacts
- `synix batch-build run/resume` — (experimental) OpenAI Batch API for 50% cost reduction

## Architecture overview

```
src/synix/
├── core/models.py          # Layer hierarchy (Source, Transform, SearchIndex, FlatFile, Pipeline)
├── sdk.py                 # SDK — programmatic access (Project, Release, search, init/open_project)
├── build/
│   ├── runner.py          # Execute pipeline — walk DAG, run transforms, cache artifacts
│   ├── object_store.py    # Content-addressed write path (.synix/objects/)
│   ├── snapshots.py       # BuildTransaction + commit_build_snapshot
│   ├── snapshot_view.py   # SnapshotView — ref-resolved reads from objects
│   ├── release_engine.py  # Release orchestration via adapter contracts
│   ├── adapters.py        # ProjectionAdapter ABC + registry
│   ├── transforms.py      # Transform base + registry
│   └── llm_transforms.py  # Bundled memory transforms + LLM helpers
├── search/
│   ├── indexer.py         # SQLite FTS5 — build, query, shadow swap
│   ├── retriever.py       # Hybrid search — keyword + semantic + RRF fusion
│   └── adapter.py         # SynixSearch projection adapter
├── cli/                   # Click CLI commands
└── templates/             # Bundled demo pipelines
```

## Pipeline definition

Pipelines are Python code with object dependencies:

```python
from synix import Pipeline, Source, SearchSurface, SynixSearch, FlatFile
from synix.transforms import MapSynthesis, ReduceSynthesis

pipeline = Pipeline("my-pipeline")
pipeline.source_dir = "./sources"
pipeline.llm_config = {"provider": "anthropic", "model": "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"}

# Parse and transform
bios = Source("bios")
work_styles = MapSynthesis(
    "work_styles", 
    depends_on=[bios],
    prompt="Infer this person's work style:\n\n{artifact}",
    artifact_type="work_style"
)
report = ReduceSynthesis(
    "report",
    depends_on=[work_styles], 
    prompt="Write team analysis from:\n\n{artifacts}",
    label="team-report"
)

# Search capability and outputs
search_surface = SearchSurface("search", sources=[work_styles, report], modes=["fulltext"])

pipeline.add(bios, work_styles, report, search_surface)
pipeline.add(SynixSearch("search", surface=search_surface))
pipeline.add(FlatFile("context", sources=[report]))
```

## Important constraints

- **SQLite only** — no external databases, content-addressed `.synix/` storage
- **No web UI** — CLI and Python SDK only
- **Fail closed** — validators/fixers require explicit `fail_open=True` for graceful degradation
- **No shared state** — tests use `tmp_path`, mocked LLMs except E2E
- **Experimental features** — batch-build and mesh require explicit `[experimental]` extras
